fixed.text:
SECTION 43B(h) OF THE INCOME TAX ACT (MSME)
(Inserted by Finance Act 2023, applicable w.e.f 01.04.2024)
A new clause (h) has been inserted after clause (g) of the section 43B by the Finance Act,
2023:
"(h) any sum payable by the assessee to a micro or small enterprise beyond the
time limit specified in section 15 of the Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
Development Act, 2006.";
shall be allowed (irrespective of the previous year in which the liability to pay such sum
was incurred by the assessee according

ay such sum
was incurred by the assessee according to the method of accounting regularly
employed by him) only in computing the income referred to in section 28 of that
previous year in which such sum is actually paid by him.
Provided that nothing contained in this section [except the provisions of clause(h)] shall
apply in relation to any sum which is actually paid by the assessee on or before the due
date applicable in his case for furnishing the return of income under sub-section (1) of
section 139 in re

income under sub-section (1) of
section 139 in respect of the previous year in which the liability to pay such sum was
incurred as aforesaid and the evidence of such payment is furnished by the assessee
along with such return.
These amendments will take effect from 1st April 2024 and will, accordingly, apply in relation
to the assessment year 2024-2025 and subsequent assessment years.

Now let us understand the Definitions of Micro and Small Enterprise as per MSMED Act,
2006.

for

Sec 15 of MSME Development Act, 2006 is reproduced below:
“Where any supplier, suppl

06 is reproduced below:
“Where any supplier, supplies any goods or renders any services to any buyer, the buyer
shall make payment thereof or on before the date agreed upon between him and the supplier
in writing or, where there is no agreement on this behalf, before the appointed day”, Where
Appointed day means the day following immediately after the expiry of the period of fifteen
days from the day of acceptance or the day of deemed acceptance of any goods or any
services by a buyer from a supplier.”
Wher

or any
services by a buyer from a supplier.”
Where Supplier means as defined in Sec 2(n) in the MSMED Act, 2006,
As per Sec 2(n), “Supplier” means a micro or small enterprise, which has filed a
memorandum with the authority referred to in sub section (1) of Section 8.

Explanation.--For the purposes of this clause,--
(i) "the day of acceptance" means,--
(a) the day of the actual delivery of goods or the rendering of services; or
(b) where any objection is made in writing by the buyer regarding acceptance of goods or
services within fifteen days from the day of the delivery of goods or the rendering of services,
the day on which such objection is removed by the supplier;
(ii) "the day of deemed acceptance" means, where no objection is made in writing by the
buyer regarding

bjection is made in writing by the
buyer regarding acceptance of goods or services within fifteen days from the day of the
delivery of goods or the rendering of services, the day of the actual delivery of goods or the
rendering of services;
Therefore, if a buyer is entering into an agreement with an MSME supplier, the due date of
payment cannot exceed 45 days from the date of actual delivery of goods or rendering
of services as the case may be. Where there is no written agreement, the due date will
be the 1

s no written agreement, the due date will
be the 16th day of actual delivery. Provided that in no case the period agreed upon
between the supplier and the buyer in writing shall exceed 45 days from the day of
acceptance or the day of deemed acceptance.
Thus, Section 15 of the MSMED Act mandates payment to MSMEs within a period of45 days in
case there is a written agreement and15 days in other cases.
Sec 16 of MSMED Act, 2006
provides that delayed payments by the buyer shall
attract compound interest at thre

the buyer shall
attract compound interest at three times the bank rate notified by the RBI. It is also
pertinent to note that section 24 of the MSMED Act contains an overriding provision over all
other Acts for the time being in force. This means that the MSMED Act prevails over all
other Acts as far as timeline of payment is concerned.

So, the following may be noted:
1. The interest is payable on the expiry of the 15th date if there is no written agreement.
2. If there is a written agreement and the same provides the period within which payment is to
be made, and the agreed period of within 45 (Forty-Five) days, then the interest shall be
payable upon the expiry of such agreed period.
3. If there is a written agreement and the same provides the period within which payment is to
be made, and the agreed period of more than 45 (Forty Five) d

d the agreed period of more than 45 (Forty Five) days, than the interest shall be
payable upon the expiry of 45 days.
Sec 17 of MSMED Act, 2006 provides that “For any goods supplied or services rendered by
the supplier, the buyer shall be liable to pay the amount with interest thereon as provided
under section 16.”
Further Sec 23 of MSMED Act, 2006 provides that  Interest not to be allowed as deduction
from income.—
Notwithstanding anything contained in the Income-tax Act, 1961 (43 of 1961), the amount of
i

Income-tax Act, 1961 (43 of 1961), the amount of
interest payable or paid by any buyer, under or in accordance with the provisions of this Act,
shall not, for the purposes of computation of income under the Income-tax Act, 1961, be
allowed as deduction.
Sec 24 of MSMED Act, 2006 provides that :
The provisions of sections 15 to 23 shall have effect notwithstanding anything inconsistent
therewith contained in any other law for the time being in force.

IMPORTANT POINTS TO CONSIDER
1. Only Micro and Small Enterprises are covered
2. Applicability of proviso of making the payment within the due date of filing return
u/s 139(1) is not applicable.
3. If payment is made within the due date of filing the return u/s 139(1) but beyind
the due date mentioned in MSMED Act, 2006, then deduction shall be available
in the year of payment and not in the year of accrual.
4. Since the Definition of Micro and Small enterprise only includes Manufacturing
or Production and S

se only includes Manufacturing
or Production and Service industry, so Traders having registeration under NIC
code of Trading will not be covered under the provisions of Sec 43B(h) of
Income Tax Act, 1961
5. Old Outstandings will not be considered in Sec 43B(h) but payment done to the
supplier will be considered on FIFO basis if proper references are not made for
payment against Invoice.
6. Sec 43B doesnot apply to the Assessee opting for Presumptive taxation. Hence
no disallowance can be made in such cases

So, the following may be noted:
1. The interest is payable on the expiry of the 15th date if there is no written agreement.
2. If there is a written agreement and the same provides the period within which payment is to
be made, and the agreed period of within 45 (Forty-Five) days, then the interest shall be
payable upon the expiry of such agreed period.
3. If there is a written agreement and the same provides the period within which payment is to
be made, and the agreed period of more than 45 (Forty Five)

nd the agreed period of more than 45 (Forty Five) days, than the interest shall be
payable upon the expiry of 45 days.
Sec 17 of MSMED Act, 2006 provides that “For any goods supplied or services rendered by
the supplier, the buyer shall be liable to pay the amount with interest thereon as provided
under section 16.”
Further Sec 23 of MSMED Act, 2006 provides that Interest not to be allowed as deduction
from income.—
Notwithstanding anything contained in the Income-tax Act, 1961 (43 of 1961), the amount of
i

Income-tax Act, 1961 (43 of 1961), the amount of
interest payable or paid by any buyer, under or in accordance with the provisions of this Act,
shall not, for the purposes of computation of income under the Income-tax Act, 1961, be
allowed as deduction.
Sec 24 of MSMED Act, 2006 provides that :
The provisions of sections 15 to 23 shall have effect notwithstanding anything inconsistent
therewith contained in any other law for the time being in force.
IMPORTANT POINTS TO CONSIDER

rendering of services;
Therefore, if a buyer is entering into an agreement with an MSME supplier, the due date of
